questions.question: In triangle ABC, angle A measures 58° and angle B measures 67°. What is the measure of angle C?
questions.explanation: The sum of angles in a triangle is 180°. So angle C = 180° − 58° − 67° = 55°.

questions.question: A right triangle has one angle measuring 34°. What is the measure of the third angle?
questions.explanation: One angle is 90° (right angle), another is 34°. The third angle = 180° − 90° − 34° = 56°.

questions.question: In an isosceles triangle, the vertex angle (the angle between the two equal sides) measures 52°. What is the measure of each base angle?
questions.explanation: In an isosceles triangle, the two base angles are equal. If the vertex angle is 52°, then 2x + 52 = 180, so x = 64°.

questions.question: Two parallel lines are cut by a transversal. One of the alternate interior angles measures 73°. What is the measure of the other alternate interior angle?
questions.explanation: Alternate interior angles are congruent when formed by parallel lines and a transversal. Both angles measure 73°.

questions.question: A triangle has sides of length 5 and 8. Which of the following could be the length of the third side: 2, 3, 12, or 13? List all that work.
questions.explanation: By the triangle inequality, the third side x must satisfy |5 − 8| < x < 5 + 8, so 3 < x < 13. Both 3 < x and x < 13 hold, giving 3 and 12 as valid options.

questions.question: In a 45–45–90 triangle, the legs each measure 6 cm. What is the length of the hypotenuse? (Express your answer in simplified radical form.)
questions.explanation: In a 45–45–90 triangle, if each leg has length a, the hypotenuse has length a√2. Here, hypotenuse = 6√2.
